pattern: Reducing each target to a mismatch bitmap turns the adjacent sign-flip into clearing ones with adjacent toggles, which a single left-to-right greedy pass costs out.
hints: There are only two possible final arrays: all ones or all minus-ones. Solve for each and take the cheaper.
Mark every position that differs from the chosen target; one operation toggles two neighbouring marks, so think about clearing marks from the left.

Sweep left to right: whenever a position is still mismatched, flip it together with its right neighbour and count the operation. If the last position ends mismatched, that target is impossible; otherwise compare the count to k.

edge_inputs.description: balanced halves cannot be unified cheaply
already all equal needs zero ops
parity makes one target unreachable but the other works

stress.description: short random +/-1 arrays with a tight budget so both feasibility outcomes occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.choice([1, -1]) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: size kept small so the BFS oracle stays fast
